entry.title: Re: Prep_extension/conflict_check taking long S4hana 2023...
entry.content:
The PREP_EXTENSION/CONFLICT_CHECK phase in the upgrade process checks for system conflicts and can be time-consuming. Factors affecting its duration include system size, add-ons, and modifications.

To expedite, ensure:

1. Adequate system resources (CPU, memory, disk space).
2. No errors or warnings in the CONFLICTCHECK.LOG file in the SUM directory's "log" folder.
3. Minimal add-ons and modifications.
4. Optimal database and network performance.

entry.title: Re: ABAP list's column width are not uniform.
entry.content:
No. What code did you use to generate this ABAP List (WRITE, ALV, etc.) Do you have full-width/double-byte characters (CJK East-Asian, etc.) in the column (*) where the shift starts?

(*) This column:

entry.title: Re: Renewal of SAP Business One

Please check this query with the RSM of the country where you are from. He/she would be the right person to guide you.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Posting Journal Entries with Tax Using SOAP Posting APIs
entry.content:
Hi Alice

Thank for you this post. 

I have a question relating to the taxes, it seems the API is by-passing 'FF 707 - Tax entered incorrectly' and allowing incorrect Tax amounts to be posted.  Does Async or Sync support this error message handling?

entry.title: Re: New Info Structure for MC.9 Report
entry.content: Hello Dominik, Thank you for replying! I have created an Info-structure and maintained its Updating Rule, then I am executing it through MCSK but getting No data output. Am I leaving any step, please guide me through this. I have also attached screenshots above.

entry.title: Re: Calling Experts: SAP IDES ON ASE as a Remote Source f...
entry.content:
The connection looks to be lost due to possible network, firewall, or configuration issues.
Ensure the HANA Platform can reach the SAP IDES server by pinging it. If it fails, resolve the network issue and check if the firewall settings on both servers are not blocking necessary communication ports.
Also, verify the ODBC configuration on the HANA Platform server, including server name, port number, and login credentials. Check if the ASE server is running and can accept new connections. Ensure the JDBC driver used by HANA is compatible with the ASE server version. Update if necessary and check, if there are any known issues or solutions related to your scenario.

entry.title: When creating a BOM we are getting the error "Enter a Number with a maximum of 3 decimal places"
entry.content:
When creating BOM for FG material we are getting an error "Enter a Number with a maximum of 3 decimal places" 

we have components quantity of 0.000020PC and system is not accepting .

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: When creating a BOM we are getting the error "Enter a...
entry.content:
The reason why the error "Enter a Number with a maximum of 3 decimal places" is raised is outlined in the KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3496095

 

The maximum allowed decimal length of the Quantity field is 3 and cannot be increased

entry.title: Re: How to add a warning message when no value is obtaine...
entry.content: Hi Raymond, thank you for your reply, I have checked the BAdI and I can work with it, but I am facing another issue. If I specify the message as an error, the message appears and everything works fine, but if I specify it as a warning, It doesn't show. Answering your question, this is the requirement I have been assigned, it seems feasible despite this last issue

entry.title: Re: SAP Authenticator app crashes on opening
entry.content:
Even I was facing the same issue, I tried to deleted and reinstalled the app. Unfortunately, when I scan TOTP QR code nothing was happing(No account was created in SAP Authenticator app).

However, here is what worked for me

1. Log into one of the Provisioning accounts using Provisioning URL

2. Enter your email id/ username and password

3. You should be able to see scan QR code option, scan the QR code on this page using SAP Authenticator app then your account should be created.

I hope it will work for you too.


Regards,
Pavana M 
TalenTeam

entry.title: Re: New Info Structure for MC.9 Report
entry.content:
Hello @Pradeep93 

First of all, you need to create 3 info structures actually - it is explained in the note 483735 - Self-defined info structures with reference S031, S032, S039 I would suggest not to copy from SAP standard info structures but to create your own from scratch. 

Then you need to fill the structures with data as explained in the notes:

  • 64636 - Procedure for setting up statistical data in the Logistics Information System (LIS).
  • 79083 - Setup of statistical data in BCO: Additional information

Finally you should use the D type info structure. Even though it won't contain any data, it will pull the data from the other info structures and calculate historical stocks on the fly.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Cold Storage enablement for SAP BW bridge in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi kurzdo, 

Thank you for this informative blog. I wanted to check, is DTO still possible in BW Bridge, or is this feature no longer present? I ask because i was working on a test system and upon creating ADSOs there i did not see the Data Tiering options.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: HANA 2.0 SPS 07 long running threads
entry.content:
The continuous running of PreprocessorPool and WebDispatcher-Main-Thread in HANA 2.0 SPS 07 should be normal. They handle text analysis/mining and HTTP requests respectively. They're designed to run non-stop for efficient task processing and request handling.

If you face issues, it's unlikely these threads are the cause. Monitor them for excessive resource consumption or other issues.

entry.title: Re: Can we use BP person created in Quality Notification...

Hello ,

Yes , its possible. choose the task processor as VW - Person Responsible to get the HR integrated Personal number.

Kumar Semmalai

entry.title: Re: Non-Deductible VAT Code and Freight in Purchase Invoice
entry.content:
Hi @FedericoS ,

Follow the below and check the behavior at your end.

In some countries you could even create an Item called "freight" and use the G/L determination to produce a similar accounting output. You would have to check whether this is compliant to the local laws and the customer's processes, of course.

The bottom line is that you should always consider localization-specifics in regards to VAT and Freight treatment. The database I'm using is UK based, so it may be different from the Italian one I suppose you are using.

KR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Requirement date of components in production order re...
entry.content: Yes, clear for me, but my question was, how to give the components a different requirement date than the operation date they are assigned to. Anyhow, we already implemented an enhancement, when the order is save, where we can manipulate this date.

entry.title: Open the posting periods 1 and 2 for account type S (G/L Accounts)
entry.content: we did a setup in Dev to setup posting periods groups, can this be transported to QA  and Prod, or must we maintain each landscape individually?

entry.title: Notification Process for Scheduled Maintenance of SAP Cloud Services
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community

I´d like to seek clarification on the notification process for scheduled maintenance of SAP Cloud services. We are SAP Build and Service partners and would like to understand how to ensure that our team receives timely email notifications about upcoming maintenance events.

Specifically, we would like to know:

  1. How can we ensure that multiple members of our team receive email notifications about scheduled maintenance well in advance?
  2. Is there a way to configure these notifications to be sent several weeks before the maintenance is set to occur?

Your guidance on this matter would be greatly appreciated as it is crucial for our planning and uninterrupted service to our clients.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Best regards,
Enrique

entry.title: Re: call API with Credentials in sap screen personas
entry.content: I want to open a api with given credentials and get the api data then store it to the container. Then validate the data in the container with screen data  in sap screen personas . I have no idea about this. I'm just a beginner in JavaScript. Pls guide me

Documentation on Roles : https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/role-collections?q=Cloud%20ALM%20Application%20help

Best Regards, 
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: New Extensibility Guide for S/4HANA is available
entry.content:
Hi @ThomasSchneider ,

what happened to Custom Extensions in SAP S/4HANA Implementations – A Practical Guide for Senior IT Leadership? Despite of being several years old, it used to be a very useful resource, so it's a pity that it was just removed without any successor...

Could you please check internally why it was removed?

entry.title: Re: Requirement date of components in production order re...
entry.content:
Nice to hear you found a solution through an enhancement!

To answer your question, there isn't a standard SAP functionality that allows you to assign a different requirement date to a component than the operation it is assigned to. The requirement date is determined by the operation's scheduling and the component's lead time.

However, as you've discovered, BAdI or enhancement can used to manipulate this date. But be aware that any changes you make could potentially impact other areas of your production process, so it's important to thoroughly test any changes.

entry.title: Output management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Output management in S/4HANA

Introduction to output management

After working with many clients in different fields, I've noticed that most businesses struggle with output management. Each client has their own idea of what output means, whether it's PDF documents, forms, emails, or iDocs. While these views are somewhat correct, I think output management is about effectively communicating with customers or vendors using the right channels and documents.

When we talk about output management, I see it as combining "output" (like print, email, iDoc) with "management" (how we set it up to reuse the functionality). Output management in SAP S/4HANA is a big improvement in how businesses generate and distribute documents. This new framework solves the complexities and inefficiencies of the many old output solutions in previous SAP systems. This blog will mostly cover the output management features in on-premises S/4HANA.

Legacy Output Solutions

Historically, SAP provided several output management tools tailored to specific business areas:

  • SD Output Control (NAST): Used primarily for sales and distribution processes.
  • FI Correspondence: Handles financial document communications.
  • FI-CA Print Workbench: Manages print jobs for contract accounts receivable and payable.
  • CRM Post-Processing Framework (PPF): Deals with post-processing activities in customer relationship management.

While these tools were effective, they required customers to learn and maintain distinct frameworks for each business area. This led to a high total cost of ownership (TCO) and varied user experiences.

What is NAST

NAST is a central component in SAP for managing and controlling the output of documents. It is part of the SAP Output Control framework and is used primarily in the Sales and Distribution (SD) module, but its functionality extends to other areas such as Materials Management (MM) and Financial Accounting (FI).

Key features of NAST include:

  • Message control
  • Output type
  • Condition record
  • Processing routine
  • Transmission medium
  • Scheduling

NAST operates using the condition technique, which is the traditional method of working in SAP.

The Need for a Unified Solution

In SAP S/4HANA projects, the fragmentation of output management tools presented several challenges:

  1. Complexity: Customers had to navigate and master different frameworks for different tasks.
  2. Maintenance: Each framework required separate setup and ongoing maintenance.
  3. User Experience: Disparate tools resulted in inconsistent user experiences across the SAP ecosystem.

What is Output Management in S/4HANA?

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA is a comprehensive framework designed to streamline the creation, formatting, and distribution of business documents. It modernizes and centralizes outdated output mechanisms, effectively meeting the diverse needs of various business processes.

Key features of S/4HANA Output Management include:

  • Centralized Configuration: Offers a single point of configuration for all output types, reducing complexity and easing maintenance. This common setup point can be used across different modules.
  • Flexibility and Scalability: Supports various output channels and formats, making it adaptable to diverse business needs and scalable for future growth.
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provides consistent and intuitive user interfaces, improving efficiency and satisfaction across different modules.
  • Simplified Maintenance: Eliminates the need for complex master data maintenance and extensive cutover activities.
  • Advanced Output Channels: Supports modern output channels like email, print, fax, and electronic data interchange (EDI), in addition to traditional methods.
  • Improved Template Management: Utilizes advanced tools for designing and managing document templates, such as Adobe Forms, enhancing document consistency and branding.

Different between NAST and Output management in S/4HAN

NAST (Legacy Output Control)

NAST is the traditional output control functionality used in earlier versions of SAP, such as SAP ECC. It manages the output of business documents through various channels like print, email, fax, and EDI. Key characteristics of NAST include:

  • Decentralized Configuration: Each application module (e.g., SD, MM, FI) has its own configuration, leading to complexity and redundancy.
  • Limited Flexibility: Customization and enhancement of output processes require significant effort and often involve custom ABAP development.
  • Condition Records: Utilizes condition records to determine when and how outputs are triggered.
  • Processing Routines: Depends on traditional forms like SAP script and Smart Forms for document formatting.
  • Separate Handling: Each business area (e.g., sales orders, invoices) has its own output determination and processing logic
  • It also covers Multiple Output Mediums and Error Handling and Monitoring

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

The new Output Management framework in SAP S/4HANA is designed to overcome the limitations of NAST and provide a more streamlined and efficient approach. Its key features include:

  • Centralized Configuration: Provides a unified setup for all output types, reducing complexity and maintenance efforts.
  • Enhanced Flexibility: Supports various output channels and formats with easier customization options.
  • Advanced Template Management: Uses Adobe Forms for designing and managing document templates, offering more sophisticated formatting and branding capabilities.
  • Output Parameter Determination: Utilizes a modern method for determining output parameters based on business context, simplifying the output determination process.
  • Improved User Experience: Consistent and user-friendly interfaces across different modules enhance operational efficiency.
  • Integration: Seamlessly integrates with other SAP S/4HANA components and external systems, improving overall document management and processing.

Key Differences

Feature

NAST (Legacy Output Control)

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

Configuration

Decentralized, specific to each module

Centralized, unified for all output types

Flexibility

Limited, requires custom ABAP development

Enhanced, easier customization

Forms and Templates

SAP script, Smart Forms

Adobe Forms

Output Parameter Determination

Condition records

Modern, context-based parameter determination

User Experience

Inconsistent across modules

Consistent and user-friendly

Integration

Separate handling for each business area

Seamless integration with S/4HANA components

Maintenance

Complex, higher total cost of ownership (TCO)

Simplified, lower TCO

 

Advantages and features of Output management

SAP S/4 HANA Output Management has the below features

  • Operates seamlessly in both cloud and on-premises environments.
  • Offers a unified solution for all output related tasks across the product and modules
    Reduce master data maintenance and relatively less time consuming for activation
  • Supports standard extensibility for configuration documents
  • Offers central monitoring tools for key users, enhancing oversight and control.

Based on Business Rule Framework (BRF), offering greater flexibility in managing output processes.

How it works?

For determining output types in transactions, we can utilize Output Parameter Determination (OPD). Maintenance of OPD can function similarly to master data or be set up via transport management. I recommend using OPD as a client-specific change. Using transports can lead to confusion, especially if other rollouts are occurring simultaneously.

Prerequisites –

There are few back-end changes you need to do it before maintenance of OPD setup.

  • Check if your application object type is active
  • Check if your Output type is available and if you want to use your own output then create new one by doing copy of standard output type.
  • Assign channel to your Output type.
  • Assign Form Template to Output Type
  • Assign Email template to output type.

To use Output management, we need to activate the required object and if we activate Output management then system will redirect output always to Output management and not to NAST.

Main parameters in OPD (Output Parameter Determination)

Below are the key parameters,

Output management is BRF based functionality which can be setup by using below 7 steps in the SAP using OPD.

Let’s check in the system –

OPD (Output parameter determination) screen

There are multiple steps (as per above screenshot) which should be maintained to determine output in billing document as per this example, here we will take billing output type as an example.

 

  1. Output Type –

This step begins by deciding whether to process the output immediately or later via a job. As shown below, for the combination of billing type F2 and output type BILLING_DOCUMENT, we can choose to process the output type immediately or through a background job (VF31F) at the time of billing document creation. Additionally, in the billing document, we can manually add the output type and set the dispatch time manually.

 

 2. Receiver –

In this step, we can determine the output type for a specific role (partner function). If we have four lines with different roles and want to consider only the first two, we will mark the Exclusive indicator 'X' for the second line. This way, the system will only consider the first two entries and ignore any entries after the second line.

3. Channel –

In this stage, the 'Channel' will be defined. The channel could be IDoc, Print, Email, etc. This stage will also include an exclusive indicator.

For instance, if the exclusive indicator 'X' is set for IDoc as shown below, the system will not search for or determine the PRINT and EMAIL channels.

4. Printer setting –

In this phase, we can specify the printer and the number of copies to be printed after creating the document. Before setting up the printer in this phase, it is necessary to install the printer in SAP.

Additionally, please be aware that the new output management system supports Printer Spool Devices limited to PDF device types only (PDFUC: PDF Unicode 1.3 / PDF1: PDF ISO Latin1 4.60+).

This restriction is detailed under the Symptoms section in note 268480 and SAP Note. 2294198

5. Email setting –

In this phase, the sender's email ID can be configured. This means that when an email is sent to the customer, the sender's email will be set up here, and the recipient's email will be the customer's email address.

Additionally, we can also establish the email template in this phase. An email template specifies the format and content that the customer will receive from us via email.

6. Email recipient –

The email address (recipient address) retrieved in the billing document is sourced from BP master data or determined through this step.

If you have configured an email address here, this setting takes precedence.

Multiple email addresses can be maintained in this determination step by separating them with ';'.
You can also define the email type code with TO, CC, BCC.

7. Form Template –

This step is intended for maintaining the form template used for each output. Ensure that the form template maintained here has been assigned to the output type in SPRO -> Cross-Application Components -> Output Control -> Assign Form Templates.

If the language is not specified in OPD, it will be retrieved from the customer master data of the output recipient.

8. Output Relevance –

This step verifies if the output item is flagged as relevant for output.

If the relevance indicator is not set to 'X - true', the output item determined in the billing document will not be considered relevant for output.

Consequently, its status will remain 'In preparation' in the billing document under the output section.

 

OPD functionalities –

Adding extra predefined standard fields

Adding new columns or fields: OPD provides default fields that cover all necessary steps we've discussed. However, often after client discussions, we realize additional parameters are needed to define our objects in OPD differently.

In OPD, there are additional fields available that we can add as columns on the main OPD screen. These can then be utilized in determination combinations as needed.

To do this, navigate to Edit -> Table settings -> Insert column -> From context data object -> Search.

Click on search

Following this step, a list of standard additional fields will be available for us to add and utilize in OPD. If the required field is still not available, we can proceed with an enhancement to add the necessary field to this list. This process requires the involvement of an ABAP specialist.

Simulation functionality

Having reviewed all seven steps, we can use them to determine the output type in the transaction.

To preview the output in simulation mode, use the highlighted option below. This allows us to simulate the output effectively.

Key points and learnings –

Pros

  • As OPD is BRF based functionality, we can import and export data using predefined template.
  • New column (fields) can be added and can be removed as per requirement.
  • SAP S/4HANA output management is recommended but not mandatory.
  • The scope of SAP S/4HANA output management is not comparable with the scope of NAST.
  • SAP S/4HANA output control is not designed to completely replace NAST.
  • Innovations will only be made in the new framework (SAP S/4HANA output management)

Cons –

  • It will only cover the output part of NAST (print, email, partly EDI).
  • The rest – like system integration, workflow, or special function – is not (and will not be) in the scope of SAP S/4HANA output control.
  • Customers will lose all NAST features when switching to SAP S/4HANA Output Control. Using S/4HANA Output Control for applications that previously relied on NAST is only advisable in exceptional cases
  • iDocs are not fully supported by the new output management. There are few restrictions as compared to NAST.
  • NO ALE is supported in Output management.
  • No tool is available for conversion from NAST to Output management.

Reference –

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/output-management-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13492419

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/d7bd4b5d70d94f09bcf99b221a7a1688.html

Check out SAP notes related to the topic -

  • SAP Note 2292539 - SAP S/4HANA output control – configuration
  • 2843926 - It is not possible to activate rules in App 'Output Parameter Determination' - SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Note 2228611 –Output Management in S/4HANA 
  • SAP Note 2248229 –SAP S/4HANA output control - download XML file
  • SAP Note 2292571 –SAP S/4HANA output control -technical setup 
  • SAP Note 2292646 –SAP S/4HANA output control -form templates with fragments 
  • SAP Note 2292681 –SAP S/4HANA output control –master form templates 
  • SAP Note 2294198 –SAP S/4HANA output control -customized forms 
  • SAP Note 2367080 –SAP S/4HANA output control -customized master forms

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA consultant certification with the perfect learning mix
entry.content:
For those who missed the summer academy of the SAP Blended Learning Academy, we offer the next opportunity to take part in flexible SAP consultant training in the fall.
In the end, the academy will enable you to complete an official SAP certification as proof of your skills. 

To become an SAP consultant:
After the one-day kick-off event, you will learn independently and in your own rhythm based on the online manuals. Once a month, you will meet again with the other participants and an instructor for the respective course, for example, to clarify any outstanding questions. After about four months, you’re ready for SAP consultant certification.
 
Discover the SAP Blended Learning Academy now as an effective way to easily train your SAP S/4HANA professional development while you’re on the job and, finally, drive your career forward with a sought-after SAP certification.

Secure a seat today:

SAP S/4HANA Consultant Academy:

  • WS4HFI Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA (FI)
  • WS4HCO Management Accounting in SAP S/4HANA (CO)
  • WS4HPP Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA (PP)
  • WS4HMM Procurement in SAP S/4HANA (MM)
  • WS4HSD Sales in SAP S/4HANA (SD)

SAP S/4HANA Integration Academy:

  • WS4INT Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Zur SAP S/4HANA Beraterzertifizierung mit dem perfekten Lernmix
entry.content:
Für all diejenigen, die die Sommerakademie der SAP Blended Learning Academy verpasst haben bieten wir im Herbst die nächste Möglichkeit, an der flexiblen SAP-Beraterausbildung teilzunehmen.
Am Ende befähigt Sie die Akademie, eine offizielle Zertifizierung als Nachweis für Ihre Fähigkeiten zu absolvieren. 

So werden Sie SAP-Berater:

Nach dem eintägigen Kick-Off-Event lernen Sie selbständig und in Ihrem eigenen Rhythmus basierend auf den Online-Handbüchern. Einmal im Monat treffen Sie sich wieder mit den anderen Teilnehmenden und einem Referenten zum jeweiligen Lernteil, um z.B. offene Fragen zu klären. Nach rund vier Monaten sind Sie bereit für die SAP-Beraterzertifizierung.
 
Entdecken Sie jetzt die SAP Blended Learning Academy als effektive Möglichkeit, Ihre SAP S/4HANA Weiterbildung ganz einfach berufsbegleitend zu machen und abschließend mit einer gefragten SAP Zertifizierung Ihre Karriere vorantreiben.

Sichern Sie sich noch heute einen Platz:

SAP S/4HANA Consultant Academy:

  • WS4HFI Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA (FI)
  • WS4HCO Management Accounting in SAP S/4HANA (CO)
  • WS4HPP Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA (PP)
  • WS4HMM Procurement in SAP S/4HANA (MM)
  • WS4HSD Sales in SAP S/4HANA (SD)


SAP S/4HANA Integration Academy:

WS4INT Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA

➡️Nächster Starttermin: 14. Oktober 2024 - 7. Februar 2025

entry.title: Re: How to open a business object in Edit mode using a cu...
entry.content: Any luck? Even I have same scenario.

entry.title: Re: Importing GLAccountBalanceFlow into sac is taking too long
entry.content: Hi @zaidanki, did you ever find a solution to this balance load performance issue? We are seeing the same behaviour just using the standard CDS with a very restricted set of data and hitting short dumps & memory outages, with <100k records.

entry.title: No calculation quantity and net value in condition contract
entry.content:
Hi everybody,

i created a condition contract but i don't display quantity, net amount and other value.

Can you help me?

Thank you.

entry.title: Consumption based customer invoicing
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a customer in the gas industry. Consumption based invoicing is standard in this industry (just like in the electricity industry for example) - so the customer pays a monthly fee and in the end of the (contract) year, the customer pays the actual used quantity of gas - already paid monthly fees.

I know that this could be processed with the consignment process in ByD. However, there is another part of the process, which complicated this process very much.

PART 1:
A gas tank is delivered and the gas filling is consigment fill-up to customer X.

PART 2:
Several contracts with individual customers (not customer X) are created for the consumption of gas in the gas tank.
These individual customers pay a monthly fee. 
At the end of the (contract) year, the actual consumption of gas is calculated.
At the invoice the already paid monthly fees are deducted and only the rest is billed. 


In case consignment is used in Part 2 again, we can't allocate the consignment to the already existing consigment of Part 1 (customer X), due to the different customer accounts.
If we create another consigment, then the double amount of the gas filling is in the system (one customer x, one for individual customers part 2) which is not correct.
If we create standard Sales Orders, ByD expects a delivery, which is not happening as the gas filling is already in the customers gas tank. 

Can anyone help in this process?
How can this be handeled in Business ByDesign? Is there a workaround in ByD?

 

Any help is highly appreciated!

Thanks already and kind regards,
Doris 

#BusinessByDesign #ByD #consumptionbasedbilling

entry.title: Send csv file from CPI to Microsoft Teams
entry.content:
Hey Experts ; 

I want to Send csv file from CPI to Microsoft Teams. Is it possible and is there any way to do that❓

entry.title: Re: Export jobs to ACDOCP from profitability & Opex are d...
entry.content:
I guess your problem is related to the plan data scope, which needs to be set during the export job creation.
Please have a look at our documentation for further information regarding the scope Examples: Financial Data Import. The plan data scope in the SAC export job works in the same way.

I hope this helps.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Turning Stress into Success!!
entry.content:
Few questions during the interviews looks so simply but it’s really difficult to answer sometimes. One of such question I faced during an interview is,

What is your greatest strength?

At my last job, we had a major project deadline approaching, and the team was feeling the pressure. Sensing the tension, I jokingly suggested we should start a 'Survivor: Office Edition' to see who could last the longest without coffee. Everyone laughed, and it broke the ice. It inspired me a bit and I organized a spontaneous 'innovation hour' where we brainstormed creative solutions. One idea was so good it saved us hours of work. So, my greatest strength I should say turning stress into success with a bit of humour and some unconventional thinking.

entry.title: 122 Movement from MIGO in EWM
entry.content:
Hi ,

I am trying to execute vendor return via MIGO.

I have an inbound delivery where I did the putaway complete, for the vendor return, do I need to complete the putaway?  

In my case, putaway is completed, I manually moved the stock to GR Bin and executed 122 movement but I am facing an error message "Unable to update inbound delivery since already distributed". Any clue?

entry.title: Re: 利用 SAP 的企业自动化实现记录到报告流程
entry.content:
There's alot of great information in this blog... I encourage you to copy the link into an Edge browser and click on English (or the language of your choice). Here's the Google Translate Introduction...

Introduction
Record to report is an important financial process that is vital to organizations seeking transparency, accountability, and strategic decision making. This comprehensive process involves the recording, processing, and reporting of financial transactions, ultimately leading to the creation of accurate and reliable financial statements. The following are the main steps and activities that summarize the record to report process...

In summary, the record-to-report process is the backbone of financial management and sets the stage for organizational success. Its impact extends beyond compliance to strategic decision-making, risk management, and stakeholder relationships. If not executed properly, it fails to promote a resilient, agile, and transparent financial framework and leads organizations to face challenges such as:

  • Delays in order processing
  • Incomplete or inaccurate customer information
  • Inventory management challenges
  • Inefficient customer communication
  • Credit and billing issues
  • Ineffective order fulfillment
  • Invoice disputes and reconciliation issues
  • Payment processing challenges
  • Customer service and support issues
  • Regulatory compliance issues

Thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Is it possible to automaticaly assign a carrier to a EWM outbound delivery ?
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to assign carriers automaticaly to EWM outbound delivery.

Is there any solution to do so, and if yes, can you give the relevant steps ?

Thanks in advance.

Eric

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

I hope this message finds you well.

I am writing to express my interest in obtaining a voucher for a free attempt at the SAP Certified Associate - Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud. I have been an ABAP developer for over 15 years and am currently looking to transition my career to focus on ABAP Cloud development.

Given my extensive experience and my desire to update my skills to align with modern SAP technologies, I believe this certification is a critical step for my professional growth. I have come across the option for people looking to switch their careers mid-life and feel that I fit this category perfectly.

Obtaining this certification will not only enhance my technical capabilities but also allow me to contribute more effectively to our team and future projects. I am committed to dedicating the necessary time and effort to complete this certification successfully.

I would greatly appreciate your support in securing a voucher for this certification attempt. Please let me know if there are any additional steps I need to take or information you require.

Thank you for considering my request.

Fabricio Fonseca.

entry.title: Re: Output management in S/4HANA
entry.content: It's a blog post, why posting to Q&A? Post there: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/bg-p/erp-blog-members

entry.title: Re: How to generate purchase order output on change of a purchase order?
entry.content:
1. To view the output beforehand, i.e. even before the save of purchase order, please use the Preview functionality available in Purchase Order. For more details on the preview feature, please check the KBA 3358605 and 3360340
  2. If you need the output to be generated and scheduled, please follow the below steps.
    • Create a custom field at purchase order header
    • Fill the above created custom field with a value (to identify it as a change)
    • In Output Parameter Determination, please adjust the configuration step with the above created custom field as a filter condition and set the Dispatch time to "Scheduled". For more information, refer to 4.3.3.2.1 Adding a New Column to a Decision Table

        Whenever an output is generated, this field is considered to pick the dispatch time of the output generated.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Activities Log Command-line Interface (CLI) to Automate Downloads + Associat
entry.content:
Hello Shawn:
'
As "usual" kudos for your great work.

Please, are you aware of any feature requests/SAP Ideas or Roadmap announcements, when it comes to Auditing in SAC? I did not find much.


To mind comes:

  1. Selecting exactly what should be audited, e.g. change update delete, which measures, etc.
  2. Retention period
  3. Being able to pause Auditing (also via Scripting) and being able to schedule such things as well via SAC Calendar, etc.


Thanks,
Andreas J.A.

entry.title: Re: Turning Stress into Success!!
entry.content:
Nicely answered.

Sometimes humor and unconventional thinking turn stress into success while working in pressurize situation and it is really required.

entry.title: Re: SAP Authenticator app crashes on opening
entry.content: SAP support said there's a bug in the authenticator app that was to be fixed by Tuesday, 16 July. In the meantime, you can authorize support to turn it off and use a different authenticator app (Microsoft is one) until they fix the bug.

entry.title: Re: Usage of Custom BAPI in LSMW
entry.content:
Hi,

 

I am also working on the same BAPI for one of my objects to create Batch Characteristics. But I am getting errors like Ïnternal error, start characteristics maintenance. Could you please advise how to overcome these errors and create characteristic.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Unable to connect SAP Content Server to S/4 Hana 2023
entry.content: Hi Symon, could you solve this problem? I have the similiar issue and can't find a solution

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting - FS items best practice
entry.content: It depends whether the context is Public Cloud or Private Cloud/On Premise. Could you specify this?

entry.title: Re: No calculation quantity and net value in condition contract
entry.content:
Hi,

I guess, you also have an error WS210 when you try to execute it in live run, unless you have maintain a settlement material in the contract. 

It seems that the system doesn't find any relevant business volume in your case for the specific contract selected. 
Review the contract, the condition record, the validity date, the business volume selection criteria associated to the contract, you may have an issue there. 
Last, you can check the source documents and CDS view to see if the business volume is part of the selection. 

Quite difficult to tell you more with the description of your post and screenshot. 

Best regards, 
Gauthier V.

entry.title: Re: Getting error while assigning report to business role...
entry.content:
Hi MD,

Please find the answer to your query in the below KBA:

2575041 - Conflict of access context during report assignment

I will paste the text from the KBA below incase it does not direct you to it.

Symptom

You would like to undestand what the Conflict column during the report assignment means, and what implications it has for reports.

When trying to assign a report to a work center view with a conflict, you may receive the error message 'Access contexts of data source and work center view do not match'.

 

Environment

  • SAP Business ByDesign
  • SAP Cloud for Customer

 

Reproducing the Issue

  1. Go to the Business Analytics work center and the view Design Reports.
  2. Search for the respective report and click on Assign.
  3. See that for some (or most) work centers, the column Conflict shows an exclamation mark, meaning there is a conflict between the access context of this report and this work center view.

 

Resolution

Every data source has a specific access context and the report will have the same context as the data source. This context is part of the data source definition and for standard data sources, it cannot be changed. In custom data sources, the access context is determined as follows:

  • Combined data sources combine the access contexts of the standard ones.
  • In joined data sources, you have to select one of the access contexts from the standard data sources.
  • Cloud data sources have no access context at all because the data comes from external systems.

Work center views also have access contexts. By default, you can only assign reports to those work center views that have the same context, however you can also override this setting as follows:

  1. Go to the work center Business Configuration and the view Overview.
  2. Search for the activity Administrator Analytics - Settings and open it.
  3. Check the Extended Access box and save.

This will allow you to assign reports to work center views even if there is a conflict. However please be aware that this will remove the access restrictions in this report.

============

If the issue persists please open a Case with SAP Technical support under the component AP-RC-ANA and we will be happy to check further for you.

 

Kind regards

Shaun

entry.title: Re: FTP to C4C Data Sources Interface failed
entry.content:
Hi Bosilina,

thanks for reaching out.

Could you be more specific on the steps needed to get to this error?

What work centers and views etc...

I look forward to your reply.

Kind regards

Shaun

entry.title: Treetable minRow count with Interactive rowMode
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am creating some integration cards of the type component. I managed to integrate treetables in them in composite views. Some of them are quite long, so I used the interactive mode, where the user can enlarge them, but initially they're just 10 rows tall.

This is how I put the table in the content (View):

<t:TreeTable
        id="treeTable"
        selectionMode="Single"

        rowMode="Interactive"

        enableColumnReordering="false"
        rows="{
            path : 'products>/nodeRoot',
            parameters: { arrayNames: ['children']}
        }">

From the API I understand that now in 1.120 (the version I'm on) the rowMode is an object with some params:
https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/api/sap.ui.table.rowmodes.RowMode
https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/api/sap.ui.table.rowmodes.Interactive

But I don't know how to set the params minRowCount or rowCount in the view rather the controller?

Regards,
Michal

entry.title: Re: How to identify report names in SAP C4C in which a pa...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thank you for your question.

When you follow the path Adaptation >> Selecting particular KUT Field >> Edit >> Reporting you can see what reports it has been assigned to. 

If the field is added to a data source it does not mean it would be available in all reports on that data source, you have the control in the report wizard to add ti as a characteristic in the report.

I hope that makes sense, if not please let me know.

Kind regards

Shaun

entry.title: Re: FTP to C4C Data Sources Interface failed
entry.content: Hi Bosilina, Please share some screenshots to help you.

entry.title: Purchase Contract Workflow Custom Development
entry.content:
This article dives into the process of creating a custom workflow for managing purchase contract approvals. While a standard workflow template exists, it might not always align perfectly with specific business requirements. This scenario necessitates a custom approach due to the following factors:

  • Approver Detail Management: The standard template likely relies on pre-defined agent IDs stored in SPRO config. However, in this case, approver details are maintained within a custom table managed by the client-side administration team. A custom workflow offers greater flexibility to accommodate this dynamic approach to approver management.
  • Attaching PDF Documents to Work Items: The standard workflow template does not have step to attach documents stored in the DMS as PDF attachments within work items to display it in the My Inbox Fiori app.
  • Deadline Monitoring for Additional Approvers:   The standard template,does not have deadline monitoring hence we would require custom template to add deadline monitoring.

Step-by-Step Guide to Build the Custom Workflow:

  1. Access Workflow Builder (T-code SWDD): Begin by launching the Workflow Builder using the transaction code SWDD.
  2. Define Basic Data: In the Basic Data section, enter a descriptive name and abbreviation for your custom workflow template.
  3. Configure Start Events: Navigate to the "Start Events" tab and define the triggering event for the workflow. The triggering object is "BUS2014," and the event is "ReleaseStepCreated," which triggers after each release level.
  4. Set Up Event Binding: The Event Binding section is crucial for specifying details about the purchase contract that the workflow will process. Here, you'll define the Event Object (Purchasing Contract), Event Creator (Purchase Contract creator/Initiator), and Release Code (to specify which release code triggers the workflow).
  5. Activate Template and Verify Linkage (T-code SWETYPV): Before proceeding, activate the custom workflow template. Then, use the transaction code SWETYPV to verify if the linkage is active for object type "BUS2014" and receiver type (your custom workflow template number). Click the detail button to confirm linkage activation.
  6. Create Steps: Construct the workflow steps using the following sequence:
    • Step 1: Get Approver Details: Create a step named "Get Approver Details" of type "Activity." Within this step, define a workflow container element named "Approver Details" to store the retrieved information.
    • Create Task: In the control tab of the "Get Approver Details" step, you can create a task. This task can also be created using the transaction code PFTC.
    •  Implement Processing Logic: Within the "Object Method" field of the task or directly in the step, choose "Class" as the Object Category. Enter the name of the class you've created specifically for this workflow. Finally, specify the method within the class that contains the processing logic for retrieving approver details from the custom table.

Note: When creating the class for the workflow, ensure it implements the following interfaces: BI_OBJECT, BI_PERSISTENT, and IF_WORKFLOW. Necessary bindings should also be established for the "Get Approver Details".

  1. Attach PDF Document: create another step named "Attach PDF". Follow similar steps as outlined for the "Get Approver Details" step to implement the logic for attaching a PDF document from the DMS (Document Management System) to the work item.

Hints for Attaching PDF Documents:

  1. Utilizing DRAD Table: The process begins by querying the DRAD table. This table serves as a link between documents and the SAP objects they are associated with. By providing the relevant information, you can retrieve the document key (dokob) and object key (objky) from the DRAD table.
  2. Extracting Document Details with CVAPI_DOC_GETDETAIL: With the document key (dokob) in hand, you can leverage the function module 'CVAPI_DOC_GETDETAIL'. This function module retrieves detailed information about the document in the internal table 'pt_files' that potentially contains information about attached files.
  3. Obtaining Document URL with SDOK_PHIO_GET_URL_FOR_GET: If 'pt_files' from CVAPI_DOC_GETDETAIL indicates the presence of attached files, you can extract the physical document ID (ph_objid) from the table. Subsequently, the function module 'SDOK_PHIO_GET_URL_FOR_GET' can be used to retrieve the URL for the specific document.
  4.  Attaching PDF using ABAP HTTP Class: Finally, the ABAP HTTP class (cl_http_client) comes into play. By creating an instance of this class using 'CREATE_BY_URL' (since the document URL is available), you can initiate an HTTP request. The 'send' method transmits the request, followed by 'receive' to capture the response. The data can be accessed as a string using 'get_data'. Once the data is retrieved, if the connection is no longer required than it can be closed by using 'close' method.
  5. Attaching data to work_item: Use function module ' SAP_WAPI_ATTACHMENT_ADD ' to add attachment to workitem.

By following these steps within the custom workflow, you can effectively retrieve and attach relevant PDF documents from the DMS.

Once the preceding steps are completed, we reach the "dialog step". Dialog steps are decision-making points within a workflow. You can either reuse a standard step or create a custom one (the reasons for choosing a custom step will be explained later).

Create a step named 'Release of purchase contract' enter the name abbreviation. 'Object category ': 'Business Object' , 'Object type' : 'BUS2014' , 'Method' : 'Single release'.Now check the object method with dialog checkbox. in the object method binding should be done for release code as the method single release in bus2014 as release code as mandatory importing parameter.

Task Binding:

  • Bind the purchase contract event object to the task in task binding.

Agent Assignment:

  1. Go to Agents --> Expression.
  2. Enter the expression: 'US' + current approver user ID (This assigns the task to the user with the specified ID prefixed with "US").

Task Properties:

  1. Go to Task Properties --> Agent Assignment for Task --> Attributes.
  2. Change the assignment type to General Task.

Verification: The Agent Assignment light should turn green, indicating successful configuration.

  •  To capture this agent assignment in the tr, use report RHMOVE30.

Activation and Workflow Trigger:

  • Activate the workflow template.
  • Creating a purchase contract will trigger the workflow.
  • A work item will be available in SBWP.

Note : Additional configuration is required in SPRO [Sap NetWeaver-> SAP Gateway Service Enablement->Content-> Workflow Setting->Maintain Task and Decision Option] to make the work item accessible within the My Inbox Fiori app. Create an entry using workflow id and step id --> [Decision keys] enter the decision text which should be displayed in the myinbox app.

Configuring Visualization (SWFVISU):

  1. To display the purchase contract details within My Inbox, navigate to transaction code SWFVISU.
  2. create an entry for the task associated with the dialog step ("Release of Purchase Contract").
  3. Configure the task visualization.
    • Select Inbox Generic App as the visualization type.
    • for the Visualization Parameter Value field, refer the standard task's TS*XXXX*172.

To Handle action for decision keys maintained in maintain task and decision key , SAP provides the Business Add-In (Badi) /IWWRK/BADI_WF_BEFORE_UPD_IB.

The standard implementation for purchase contract approval serves as a reference       point. You can access its details through enhancement implementation         MM_PUR_CTR_APPROVE_ACTIONS and Badi implementation MM_PUR_CTR_APPROVE_ACTIONS (class implementation CL_MM_PUR_CTR_APPR_ACTION_BADI).

With the workflow template development complete, the workflow will now automatically trigger after every purchase contract creation. This will generate a corresponding work item that will be delivered directly to the designated approver's My Inbox Fiori app. This streamlined approach facilitates efficient purchase contract management and approval processes.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

Thank you for opening this learning opportunity for women.

I am interested in joining the digital skills initiative by SAP Learning for several reasons:

Firstly, as a woman, I am eager to be part of an initiative that supports underrepresented groups. This program offers an inspiring opportunity to enhance my skills and contribute to the community of women in tech.

Secondly,I am falling in love with SAP HANA and I'm eager to take a free certification attempt because I believe it will help me learn more and become even better at what I do and I am committed to continuous learning and personal development.

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to the possibility of participating in this transformative initiative.

Best Regards,

Hung.

entry.title: Posting to cost centers of different company code
entry.content: Dears I would like to know the  applicability to goods issue from company code A  (warehouse stock ) with  201 to another  cost center  related to another company code ?

entry.title: Re: Costing Variants | Standard | Plan | Actual- Usage in Production Order
entry.content: forwarded to experts

entry.title: Re: Treetable minRow count with Interactive rowMode
entry.content:
You can set it as element in the XML-View like this (works in this example):

 

<mvc:View
        xmlns="sap.ui.table"
        xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
        xmlns:mode="sap.ui.table.rowmodes" ...>
            <TreeTable ...>
                <rowMode>
                    <mode:Interactive minRowCount="10" />
                </rowMode>
            </TreeTable>
</mvc:View>

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Adaptation Transport Organizer
entry.content:
I might be mistaken, but keep in mind that you must create the Local and the Sandbox package with the LOCAL Software Component upfront so before you execute the Wizard via S_ATO_SETUP.

I was under the impression, that the wizard will create the packages for me.. 🧙

entry.title: Migrate Your Data - Which Migration Object to use for mass creation of Articles
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Can you please advise, which Migration Object to use for mass creation of Articles.

While using migration object 'Product' which creates the Materials and those are available in MM03. However when trying to check the same in MM43, getting error message as "You cannot use the material for retail (MAW1 missing)". 

Table MAW1 (Material Master: Default Fields and Special Retail Fields) has no entries.

Please advise.

Regards,

SureshRT

entry.title: Re: Link attachement to output in billing document

entry.title: Re: No processor in submitted documents on Verify General...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

Did you have a look at this knowledge base article (KBA): https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3004412

Did you follow the workflow described in this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/workflow-to-verify-general-journal-entries-per-level-by-amount/ba-p/13549503

Did you read the extensive SAP Help Portal documentation, especially the information about "Enabling and Extending Workflows" (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/13c9c4a304a4489fa3fd5440fa1cdac0.html?locale=en-US)

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Quality inspection result recording failing in SAP Di...
entry.content: You can only create Inspection Points in Order POD, not in Work Center POD, as stated in the help entry I previously shared. So, as far as I understand, if you have Inspection Points configured properly for your scenario and run it in Work Center POD without creating Inspection Points, Inspection Results Recording should always fail with "You must fill in the user fields(s)" error, as a Inspection Point would be missing.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration: How to call DocuSign from iFlow: Authentication
entry.content:
Hi @CarlosRoggan  , 

Thank you for the excellent blog. I have tried to replicate the same steps and was able to generate the JWT Token. However, while using that token, I encountered the following error:

 

json
 
{
"Error": {
"HttpStatusCode": 401,
"UserMessage": "Access Denied",
"DeveloperMessage": "Access Denied",
"ErrorCode": 103,
"ReferenceId": "d0cd7159-0b27-4e10-bf1d-a8dec47c2df2"
}
}

I have also checked the generated JWT token on jwt.io to validate the payload details, and it is providing the following information:

"\u0001\n\u0000\u0000\u0000\u0001\u0000\u0005\u0000\u0007\u0000\u0000\u0018#鑥�H\b\u0000\u0000��J���H\u0002\u0000Z\u0001�)���B�E�N�z�(\u0015\u0000\u0001\u0000\u0000\u0000\u0018\u0000\u0002\u0000\u0000\u0000\u0005\u0000\u0000\u0000\u001d\u0000\u0000\u0000\r\u0000$\u0000\u0000\u0000c7a7fd22-5fff-4501-a447-933331026863\"\u0000$\u0000\u0000\u0000c7a7fd22-5fff-4501-a447-933331026863\u0012\u0000\u0001\u0000\u0000\u0000\u0006\u0000\u0000\u0000jwt_br#\u0000$\u0000\u0000\u0000c7a7fd22-5fff-4501-a447-933331026863" 

Could you please help me on what might be the reason.

FYI:  https://authuat.springcm.com/api/v2/{Docusign API Account ID}/account -- this is the API I am trying to access.

Thanks & Regards,

Vinod Kumar

entry.title: Performance issues with standard GLAccountBalanceFlow CDS when loading SAC
entry.content:
Hi folks.

When using GLAccountBalanceFlow (OData service UI_FINS_GLAccountFlow) to load transactional data from S/4 into SAP Analytics Cloud, we are noticing that the performance is significantly worse than all other transactional CDS views we are using.

We are applying all recommended filters you would expect, such as Chart of Account, Controlling Area, Movement Type, Fiscal Period etc. and despite a relatively low data volume (<100k) the performance is still unacceptably bad. Attempting to run will hang for several hours and then raise short dumps due to memory outage.

If this is the CDS view that is intended as the SAP standard solution for loading closing Balances, something is going wrong. Are there any recommended steps or prerequisites to get this to work better? It is only this one CDS that seems to be having such issues.

Kind Regards

entry.title: Re: Message No. QV164 - Inspection Lot created via Action...
entry.content:
Hello ,

Check the below transactions to know whether do you got any Error while saving it in the database. 

1. Business Workplace (T-Code: SBWP) - Whether any messages triggered with tile "Update was canceled"

2. ABAP Runtime Errors ( ST22) 

3. Update Request (SM13)

Regards,

Kumar Semmalai

entry.title: Re: Create Unit Cost Estimates Without Quantity Structure
entry.content: forwarded to experts

entry.title: automation not getting executed despite unattended mode of bot and environment assignment to bot
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a business process that calls automation and an agent in unattended mode with project and bot both deployed in public environment. Despite having all checks in place, the automation continues to stay in 'started' mode and overall process in 'RUNNING' mode. 

May I know what is missing to make the automation work ?

Regards,

Shweta.

entry.title: Re: Accept/Reject Operations in SAM while EAM_PHASE_MODEL" enabled
entry.content: Hi, please provide some more information about your use case. Is the user logged in as a Technician or a Supervisor? Are the Accept and Reject mobile statuses been configured in the Config panel in the Mobile Status State machine?

entry.title: Re: Can I dynamically switch between Screen Personas base...
entry.content:
Thank you for the help! For this screen it is looking like I will need to use different personas as some of the same fields need to be labeled differently based on the scenario.

Could you help me to understand how to reference a field on the screen for a script? I found an example shown below where a variable on-screen was is identified using session.findByID. Where do I find the quoted variable location for the script (line 2)?

//Set Variable for Order Type
var Order=session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTAXI_TABSTRIP*/tabpT\\01/ssubSUBSCREEN_BODY:SAPMV45A:4301/ctxtVBAK-AUART").text;

//Switch flavor if order type is = ZFOC
if (Order =="ZFOC");
{session.utils.changeFlavor("00505686770A1ED78DA1150E838D9420");}

SAP Screen Personas

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – App Variants are the EASY way to control App Features
entry.content:
Hello,

We've created multiple variants of the same App. It has added as many link as we've created in the list of semantic links in UX. For example, we created 5 variants for maintenance notification search, users have now 5 new links to display maintenance notifications in their navigation.

How can we avoid users to be annoyed by those links?

entry.title: E-Learning: SAP Analytics Cloud Enterprise Planning
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud has set a new standard for enterprise planning and analysis by seamlessly integrating business intelligence and planning capabilities in a single cloud-based solution.

We are delighted to bring you a new SAP Enterprise Support E-learning, which is designed to guide you through a full interactive overview on Enterprise Planning with SAP Analytics Cloud.
The goal of the E-Learning is to present the core features of Enterprise Planning with SAP Analytics Cloud, and to demonstrate integration with Business Intelligence and Augmented Analytics.  


In this E-Learning we provide you with a sample dataset so that you can follow along in your own tenant at your own pace. There is no time limit so you can work through the content in a flexible manner. 

Agenda:

Unit 1: We have some initial theory with the introduction to SAP Analytics Cloud Planning.
We explain the differences between the planning model vs analytic model and demonstrate the planning model creation.

Unit 2: We begin by creating a basic Story. This includes learning about model maintenance, importing data, and version management.

Units 3 & 4: These are core units to the E-Learning where we cover making changes within the planning data. 
We work on some data entry tasks and discuss data locking, using the data audit and the planning panel tool.  You will also gain an understanding on data actions including advanced format data actions.

Unit 5: We showcase working with different currencies including virtual and persisted currency conversion. 

Unit 6: Finally we tackle creation of a predictive scenario. You will gain insights on version management for predictive planning, forecast layout and creation of a predictive forecast.

Before you begin:

This E-Learning is brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.
Before you can access the E-Learning link, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required with your S-User ID. There is no additional cost involved. 
A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

ACCESS NOW


If you would prefer to join us on a live virtual classroom enablement session on SAP Analytics Cloud Planning, please do check out my other blog about SAP Analytics Cloud - Expert Guided Implementation.


Further Support and Empowerment via SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps


As mentioned above, this topic is brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support Value Map for SAP Analytics Solutions.
Compliment your e-Learning experience by joining the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.
We are providing continued support and collaboration, along with further enablement materials such as Learning Journeys, best practice documents, webcasts, and e-learnings.
The Value Map is a great way for you to access to your service and support entitlements, along with further support and empowerment for SAP Data, Analytics and Planning solutions.

Join the SAP Analytics Solutions value map today.
* S user required

 

Quicklinks

    • Practical Guide: How to build SAP Analytics Cloud stories using SAP Datasphere
    • SAP Datasphere Fundamentals: Expert Guided Implementation
    • SAP Analytics Cloud - Expert Guided Implementation
    • SAP Extended Planning and Analysis (xP&A): eLearnings

entry.title: Re: SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework
entry.content:
Hi  @iryna_bintsarovska 

Being open, the Global University Alliance and Essential Metamodels offer higher fidelity as the metamodel detail can be viewed, whereas LeanIX is proprietary and hence may be encapsulated.

Svyatoslav Kotusev's resources are most helpful.

Considering the perception of reality, EA could appropriate the outer layers of Saunders' Research Onion, e.g. Understanding research philosophy and approaches to theory development (researchgate.net). Ontology (the 'what': metamodel, reference architecture), epistemology (the 'how', referring to the SAP EAF Metro Map in this case), and axiology (principles and constraints) are exhibited in EA, alongside philosophical positions such as pragmatism, which is my view is the most appropriate for EA, as it cuts across positivism and interpretivism, and requires abductive reasoning. Hopefully, we won't baffle others reading this thread - my apologies! (NB: Saunders' Onion is textbook reading for undergraduate and graduate business students, so it isn't rocket science - phew!)

Meanwhile, we can make excellent use of the SAP EAF, enhanced as @Rene_de_Daniel describes in his original post above. The SAP EAF brings EA off the page so it can be used in real life and not only for SAP, as it has broader considerations.

One of my endeavours is to bring the above into EA with SAP EAF as the exemplar. @iryna_bintsarovska, so we don't bore or lose everybody else along the way (unless they are interested), perhaps you and I could have a private chat to move it forward.

Thanks,

Simon

entry.title: Re: Repeat value from previous cell when it is blank per...

entry.title: Re: Regarding Unable to Activate with error messages in 'Manage Contract' App
entry.content: forwarded to experts

entry.title: Re: How to re-forward transport requests from test to prod in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: I am afraid you used an incorrect tag for your question: Transports in S/4HANA Cloud are not performed using SAP Cloud Transport Management. Therefore experts following this tag (like me) will not be able to support. The error message seems to be quite application specific (not technical), so you might try a tag referring to the application in question.

entry.title: Re: Revenue Recognition - Cost-Based Percentage of Completion Methods with different WBS
entry.content: forwarded to experts

entry.title: NF-e Nota Técnica 2024.001 Versão 1.10: Atualizações recentes na Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e)
entry.content:
(English version here) 

Olá pessoal,

O time de SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil entregou atualizações relacionadas à Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e). As atualizações estão conforme a Nota Técnica 2024.001 versão 1.10 de NF-e. A nova versão está prevista para entrar em produção em 2 de setembro de 2024, segundo a SEFAZ.

NF-e Nota Técnica 2024.001 Versão 1.10

As novas atualizações para notas fiscais eletrônicas (modelo 55) substituem o processo de denegação pelo processo de rejeição e adicionam a opção 4 (Simples Nacional - Microempreendedor Individual - MEI) no campo Código de Regime Tributário.

Você pode implementar essas alterações na opção de emissão de fatura de saída para o Brasil seguindo as instruções da SAP Note 3487670 e SAP Note 3488179.

Se você usa a opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil, siga as instruções na SAP Note 3488179.

Você também pode implementar as SAP Notes acima na solução SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP NFE).

Blog posts relacionados:

  • Dashboard do Usage Analytics
  • Como navegar facilmente nas páginas do SAP Help Portal para Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • Como navegar facilmente nas páginas do SAP Help Portal para Inbound Nota Fiscal

Para obter um passo a passo sobre como usar o SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil, consulte as seguintes páginas do portal SAP Help:

  • Opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil
  • Opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-IN
  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Acesse o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Consulte o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar esta ferramenta.

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Obrigado e até a próxima,
Thiago

Tags: brazil, brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, electronic nota fiscal, outbound, nf-e, grc, technical note, 2024.001, crt, nt 2024.001, j_1bcrt, changes for individual microentrepreneur, documento fiscal eletronico, electronic fiscal document, simples nacional, microempreendedor individual, mei, micro empreendedor individual, código de regime tributário, crt, denial, rejection, status code, denied, denegação

entry.title: Re: Pick list date must be between order date and cancell...
entry.content: Hi, This SAP note is not helpful for me. It's relevant under the pick list number issue related.

entry.title: Cloud Identity services neo migration to cloud foundry
entry.content:
Hi All,


I have a general question. We have a CIS tenant in the NEO environment (Germany/Netherlands). According to SAP, for other services in the BTP environment, new services are not being offered in the NEO environment; they are only available in the multi-cloud Foundry environment (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud). For example, for CPI, SAP recommends customers to migrate from NEO CPI to the Cloud Foundry Integration Suite, the same applies to API management and mobile services.


Should we do the same for the CIS system as well? I have not found any blogs or documentation discussing the migration of CIS services running on the SAP NEO Data Center to the Cloud Foundry environment. If there is any information available, please share it, and what is the last supported date for the CIS system in NEO environment.

 

 

Thanks and Regards

Zameer Ahamad

entry.title: Re: How to trigger Build Process Automation (BPA) workflo...
entry.content: In the end you are just calling an API so you could do it from anywhere you can make HTTP calls (helps to use destinations but not required).

entry.title: Re: Fiori LP App LP Tile throws "Unable to set property '...
entry.content:
Thank you @junwu for reply, I am aware the system is rather outdated. However I should think it still must be possible to create and run apps on it. 

Some tweaks I made which helped:

1. in SAP BAS, the minimum SAPUI5 version was lowered to 1.71.57

2. In LPD_CUST, URL for the service was corrected, from /default_host/sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/<z_my_service> to just /sap/bc/ui5_ui5/sap/<z_my_service>

3. In LP Admin, in target configuration for my tile, the app type was changed from "SAPUI5 Fiori app" to "SAP Fiori App with LPD_CUST"

At the moment the app at least runs from the launchpad, albeit not looking as it did at minimum SAPUI5 1.121

entry.title: Re: SAPSetVariable Does Not Work
entry.content: First of all, thank you for taking the time to respond. I tried all versions one by one. It refreshes the data, but the prompt part does not work. I couldn't understand the reason. When I searched it on the internet, it actually seems very easy. But I can't figure out what's missing.

entry.title: Re: No processor in submitted documents on Verify General...
entry.content: Hi Henrike, thank you for your answer. This problem has been solved though, i forgot to add it here. There was a mistake in workflow configuration, once it's solved, the problem has gone. Thanks for the helpful sharing again!

entry.title: Re: BTP Integration Suite API Management and Stage Releva...
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

as far as I know, API Management content is also packaged as an MTA (like Cloud Integration content), so that speaking purely technical, MTA extensions should also work for API-M transport requests. 

However, I have no idea how an extension descriptor for API-M would look like and if it would be processed by API-M in a comparable way to Cloud Integration...

Kind regards
Harald

entry.title: Re: Outbound Service Consumption Model Error - Failed to change codepage (OData V4)
entry.content: forwarded to experts

entry.title: FM HANA Implementation Problem with Fund Reservations
entry.content:
I am trying to activate the FM module in HANA by copying my customizing from an ECC client.

I activated the new Manage Budget Structure (table FMB_FMADDRESS) and have no problem uploading my addresses and posting budget including the release strategy. However, when I post a Fund Reservation, the document does NOT trigger the  FMDERIVE, does NOT check the Posting Address (is missing the Funded Program) and does NOT update table FMIOI. Document posts with no error message.

The Fund Reservation is created with no relation whatsoever to FM. When I execute FMN5N, I get I message  saying the Commitment Item in the document is NOT financial cat 30, but is not true, the financial category in the commitment item is 30 / 3. I have gone up and down the customizing and nothing is missing, the very same customizing works perfectly in ECC so at this point I have no idea what the problem might be, and it is very frustrating because the document post with NO error messages and the message I get from FMN5N is not even true.

I would appreciate any feedback.

entry.title: Re: BUPA_SEND vs DRFOUT - what is the difference and when to use which one?
entry.content: DRFOUT does not respect any DRFF filters by design once you specify a manual filter criteria in DRFOUT. If you wish to replicate objects manually while also considering filters set in DRFF, this has to be done in the MDG UI using the "Replicate by <object> selection" functionality.
